When spacing is horizontal, use `wrap` to control whether items wrap automatically. The default is `false`.

#### Alignment
Choose the best label alignment based on specific goals and constraints.
form.basic:
### Typical Form
Includes various form items, such as input fields, selectors, switches, radio buttons, checkboxes, etc.
form.customvalid:
#### Custom Validation Rules
Use custom validation rules to complete form validation.
form.dynamicvalid:
#### Dynamic Validation Rules
Execute different validation rules based on different conditions.
form.length:
#### Auxiliary Validation
Validate certain data types.
form.valid:
#### Form Validation
Help users discover and correct errors as early as possible, while preventing mistakes.
form.withmodal:
#### Multi-form Linkage
Outside the Form, submit the form via `submit` from the outside. Conversely, it's recommended to use `<Button htmlType="submit" />` to call the native submission logic.

grid.alignWhiteSpace:
### Complex Data Detail Page (Alignment & Whitespace)
Effect of a Descriptions list, but automatically adjusts display density on different screens.
grid.architecture:
### Layout Hierarchy Design (Architecture)
- Layer 1: Layout series — the main page skeleton (Header + Sider + Content).

- Layer 2: Grid series — the primary 2D layout for the content area (stat cards, Bento chart matrix).

- Layer 3: Row/Col — simple 1D proportional division (e.g., two side-by-side tables at the bottom).

- Layer 4: Flex — extremely fine-grained content alignment (card title + icon).
grid.autoFillMinWidth:
### Dashboard Card Layout (Auto-fill + Min-Width)
No need to manually set breakpoints. Rely on `itemMinWidth` to let the container automatically increase or decrease the number of columns based on its width.

> When `itemMinWidth` is set, the `cols` parameter becomes ineffective. This is a content-driven layout method, perfect for image galleries or card lists, ensuring cards maintain a suitable width without becoming too crowded during container resizing.
grid.basic:
### Basic Usage

> The `offset` logic of Grid is based on `grid-column-start`. In `dense` fill mode, `offset` might cause unexpected "gap-filling" behavior.
grid.bento:
### Image Gallery / Masonry (Bento Grid Style)
Asymmetric layout.
grid.breakpointFallback:
### Complex Form Responsiveness (Breakpoint Fallback)
The logic for finding breakpoints up or down. For example, if `md` is defined but `lg` is not, can the system correctly apply the `md` value?
grid.fixedRowsAreas:
### Holy Grail Layout / Admin Panel (Fixed Rows & Areas)
The vertical control power of `rows` and `rowSpan`.
grid.footerStrategy:
### Responsive Footer / Menu (Footer Strategy)
Extreme compression from "multi-column vertical arrangement" to "single-column folding".
grid.heroSection:
### Hero Section Overlay Layout (Layering)
The overlapping capability of `grid-column-start` and `grid-row-start`, with text floating over a specific part of an image.
grid.suffixDisplayNone:
### Responsive Hiding & Forced Sorting (Suffix & Display None)
`span: 0` completely removes the DOM placeholder, and `suffix` spans across all dynamic items.

You can set the icon's type, size, and color via the `type`, `size`, and `color` attributes, respectively. You can also use the `spin` attribute to achieve a rotating animation effect.

By setting the `icon` attribute, you can add an icon to the input field, which is only effective for `input`. This allows for quick implementation of features like password visibility toggle or search.

layout.basic:
### Typical Layout
A typical page layout.
layout.layout1:
### Top-Middle-Bottom Layout 
The most basic "Top-Middle-Bottom" layout.
The main navigation is usually placed at the top of the page, from left to right: logo, primary navigation items, auxiliary menus (user, settings, notifications, etc.). Content is typically placed within a fixed width (e.g., 1200px), ensuring a stable page layout unaffected by the user's terminal display. The top-to-bottom structure aligns with users' vertical browsing habits and is a classic website navigation model. This layout improves information display efficiency in the main work area but sacrifices some vertical space. Additionally, due to the horizontal space limitation of the navigation bar, it is unsuitable for information structures with many primary navigation items.
layout.layout2:
### Top-Side Layout
A page with top navigation and a sidebar, commonly used for showcase websites.
layout.layout3:
### Top-Side Layout - Full Width
Also features top navigation and a sidebar, but differs in that there are no side margins, commonly used for application-type websites.
layout.layout4:
### Fixed Sidebar
When content is long, a fixed sidebar provides a better user experience.
layout.layout5:
### Side Layout / Collapsed Mode
A two-column side layout. When horizontal page space is limited, the side navigation can be collapsed.
The side navigation uses a left-right structure on the page, with the main navigation usually fixed on the left side and auxiliary menus placed at the top of the work area. Content adapts to the browser terminal, improving the utilization of horizontal space, but the overall page layout is less stable. This navigation mode has strong hierarchical scalability, allowing first, second, and third-level navigation items to be displayed more smoothly and cohesively. The side navigation can also be fixed, enabling users to quickly locate and switch their current position during operation and browsing, offering high operational efficiency. However, this type of navigation sacrifices some horizontal space for page content.

menu.accordion:
### Expand Only Current Parent Menu
Clicking a menu item collapses all other expanded menus, keeping the menu focused and clean.
menu.basic:
### Top Navigation
Horizontal top navigation menu.

The table supports row and column spanning; in renders, use cell props colSpan or rowSpan. When set to 0, the cell will not render.
table.customHeader:

upload.accept:
### Upload File Types
Use the `accept` attribute (a native HTML input attribute) to restrict the types of files that can be uploaded.
`accept` supports two types of string values:
- A set of file extensions (recommended), such as `.jpg`, `.png`, etc.
- A set of MIME types for files. Refer to the [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Basics_of_HTTP/MIME_types/Complete_list_of_MIME_types) documentation.

For example, to only allow users to upload PNG and PDF files, `accept` can be written as: `accept = '.pdf,.png'` or `accept = 'application/pdf,image/png'` (simply connect the MIME types for PNG and PDF with a comma).
upload.avatar:
### Upload Avatar
When `limit` equals the number of uploaded files, the file selection component will not be displayed.
upload.basic:
### Click to Upload
Classic style. When the user clicks the button, a file selection dialog pops up.
upload.custom:
### Manual Upload / Custom Properties
By setting `data` and `headers`, you can add custom upload properties.
When `autoTrigger='false'`, selecting a file will not automatically trigger the upload. You need to manually call the `upload` method on the ref to trigger it.
`name` is the uploaded filename.
upload.directory:
### Upload Folder
By setting `directory` to `true`, you can support uploading all files from a folder.
upload.draggable:
### Drag and Drop Upload
Set `draggable='true'` to enable drag-and-drop functionality.
upload.exceed:
### Upload Restrictions
`limit` restricts the number of uploads. The `minSize` and `maxSize` attributes allow you to customize file size limits for uploads.
upload.fileList:
### Upload Multiple Files
By setting the `multiple` attribute, you can support selecting and uploading multiple files simultaneously.
If not set, only one file can be uploaded by default.
upload.pictures:
### Photo Wall
Set `type = 'picture'` to allow users to upload images and display thumbnails in a list.
upload.transform:
### Pre-upload Image Processing
Use `transformFile` to process the file before it is uploaded, for example, to compress it.
